authority: local Bundjil decision/routing writes, read-only GitHub repository-settings readback, Git commit and push to main, and remote-ref readback; no provider, workflow, secret, deployment, or GitHub-setting mutation

checks.check: decision schema, source provenance, and provider inventory
checks.result: Five records pass the canonical Draft 2020 schema, exact task-map equality, instance-relative receipt linkage, and five base-source digests; read-only GitHub Actions settings reproduced with explicit limitations.
Twenty-four focused tests preserve exact admission, occurrence isolation, duplicate rejection, and stale-entry detection; no replacement was admitted.

The evaluator exposed one-way consumer mapping, a self-deadlocking Eve deferral, wrong runbook paths, and understated Claude mutation risk. Corrected central dependencies/scopes and local records then received PASS.

Effect setup, boundary and skill audits, 29 tooling tests, Ultracite, lint fixtures, Knip, five workspace typechecks, and 250 package/app tests passed with a schema-valid synthetic Executor test configuration and no provider call.
After push and fetch, local HEAD and origin/main both resolved to 42b4ab15438dd5d6c736564c1cd9a88e932d61b6.

postcondition: Bundjil HGI-308 has four accepted reversible local defaults and one nonblocking Eve live-state deferral; HGI-302 through HGI-306 directly name the records they implement.
rollback: Revert the validation-receipt commit, then semantic commit 42b4ab15438dd5d6c736564c1cd9a88e932d61b6; revert the matching central HGI-302 through HGI-306 pointers while preserving HGI-300/HGI-301.
limitations: GitHub Actions settings do not prove workflow execution, token issuance, PR-comment authority, effectiveness, cost, or convergence.
Vercel, Executor, Sendblue, Upstash, and secret-binding readbacks remain unavailable or intentionally unattempted pending target-owned authority.
HGI-308 commits use the disclosed per-command unsigned fallback because the configured 1Password signing agent remained unavailable; content, tree, ancestry, push, and remote identity are verified.
nonClaims: No provider, workflow, GitHub setting, secret, deployment, webhook, message, credential, production, release, or publication state was mutated.
The Eve live-state deferral and current Claude workflow are not accepted evidence of provider actuality or safe automation.
